subject: Chemistry
title: Agent Orange: The Defoliant Disaster
description: One-minute skill: Agent Orange: The Defoliant Disaster.

hook.text: The US sprayed 20 million gallons of Agent Orange over Vietnam. It destroyed forests—and poisoned millions.

buildup.text: Agent Orange was a herbicide mix of 2,4-D and 2,4,5-T designed to strip jungle canopy and expose enemy positions.

discovery.text: The 2,4,5-T was contaminated with TCDD dioxin—one of the most toxic synthetic chemicals ever made.

twist.text: Dioxin persists in soil for decades. It causes cancer, birth defects, and immune disorders in exposed populations.

climax.text: Three million Vietnamese and thousands of US veterans suffered health effects. Lawsuits continued for 50 years.

punchline.text: A herbicide contaminated with dioxin. Forests died. People suffered for generations.

quiz.question: What made Agent Orange so harmful to humans?
options: It was contaminated with TCDD dioxin, a persistent toxic chemical
The herbicide itself was directly toxic to human cells
